title: UDLM DecisionRecord (ADR/DR)
description: Machine-validatable DecisionRecord — the Knowledge-family ADR/Decision-Record entity (entities/knowledge-family.md §4.5; SPEC-DESIGN §29). Records the WHY of a decision (the rationale resolving a Finding about a Capability/dependency/spec element) as a first-class, queryable record. An ADR = a DecisionRecord whose anchor is architecture; process/enablement decisions are DecisionRecords too. ADR's 'supersede, never edit' IS UDLM immutability + supersedes. data-model-core §2 [D8.6].

handle.description: e.g. 'ADR-017'-style within an owning scope; advisory.

tenant_uuid.description: Owning tenant. OPTIONAL — a Knowledge artifact (spec-level ADR) is platform/global; a tenant's own decision records carry its tenant_uuid.

rationale.description: The prose ADR narrative — kept FIRST-CLASS; the structure is an envelope around it, never a replacement (knowledge-family §4.5).

scope.description: The anchor's scope — determines the CANONICAL-gating mechanism: architecture → use-case/conformance validation; policy → Policy-Engine + Shadow Mode; provider → attestation + conformance. There is no single validation runner.

question_refs.description: Finding/question UUIDs or handles this record addresses (M:N — one record can close a class).

abstraction_decomposition.description: REQUIRED: the Data · Policy · Provider aspects of the decision (the three foundational abstractions; SPEC-DESIGN §29). A record that can't name all three must mark one 'n/a, because…'. A fully-scoped decision names all three.

validation_evidence.description: OPTIONAL — where the decision is testable, the passing validation that earned CANONICAL (scope-appropriate: UC/conformance | Policy-Engine+Shadow | attestation). Non-validatable decisions (e.g. a naming choice) may be CANONICAL without it.

about.description: references — Capability / TaxonomyTerm / spec-element anchor(s) this record justifies (by uuid/handle).

supersedes.description: DecisionRecord uuid(s) this one supersedes (ADR 'never edit, supersede').

state.description: Curation lifecycle = ADR status: PROPOSED → UNDER_REVIEW → CANONICAL (the authoritative WHY) → re-OBSERVED if premises drift; DEPRECATED when superseded.

provenance.description: Who/what proposed this — human + model/prompt version (knowledge-family §4.5).

allOf.comment: A CANONICAL record whose action is a substantive resolution SHOULD carry either validation_evidence or an explicit non-testable note — enforced as: CANONICAL + testable-method present implies passed true.
